page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance featuring four dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 15.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core, and lacks local storage. It includes a single NVIDIA T4 GPU based on the Turing architecture, which provides 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps under a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between general-purpose compute and GPU-accelerated tasks. Standard CPU and memory benchmarks, such as stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark, consistently rank in the bottom 25% or bottom 10%, indicating weak single-core and multi-core processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large-block read speeds categorized as poor. Conversely, the integrated NVIDIA T4 GPU delivers strong performance in specialized workloads. LLM inference benchmarks for small (135M) and medium (7B) models show top-tier prompt processing speeds and strong text generation capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile that balances a lower-tier CPU and memory allocation with a dedicated enterprise GPU. This resource density makes it a practical option for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not demand high-performance host CPU processing or large system memory pools. Appropriate workloads include small-to-medium LLM inference, basic machine learning model deployment, and lightweight GPU-accelerated graphics or compute tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server configured with four dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs, 15.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks demonstrate a distinct tradeoff: standard CPU and memory operations score in the bottom 25%, whereas GPU-driven LLM inference tasks for small and medium models achieve top-tier and strong ratings. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-dependent workloads that do not require high-performance host CPU resources. It is best suited for small-to-medium LLM inference, basic machine learning deployment, and lightweight parallel computing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU. It offers top-tier LLM inference speeds despite lower-tier CPU performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c4g1.xlarge features 4 Intel vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU, delivering strong LLM inference performance.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance pairing dedicated Intel processors with an NVIDIA T4 GPU for efficient small-to-medium model inference workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
One NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of Turing-architecture VRAM
15.0 GB of system memory and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Suited for machine learning inference and lightweight GPU compute
